directive.explicit_directive: Set the module in evaluation mode. This has an effect only on certain modules. See the documentation of particular modules for details of their behaviors in training/evaluation mode, i.e. whether they are affected, e.g. Dropout, BatchNorm, etc.

api_misuse.explanation: To calculate the accuracy in evaluation mode, set the model to evaluation mode. This is important as the network as dropout layers.

directive.explicit_directive: If provided, a Tensor which is of the same type as y, and has a shape broadcastable with condition and y.

api_misuse.explanation: When x and y are provided, those tensors should have the same type.

479.impact: InvalidArgumentError: cannot compute SelectV2 as input #2(zero-based) was expected to be a int32 tensor but is a int64 tensor [Op:SelectV2] name

directive.explicit_directive: Initial data for the tensor. Can be a list, tuple, NumPy ndarray, scalar, and other types.

api_misuse.explanation: Cannot infer data type from none type.

501.impact: RuntimeError: Could not infer dtype of NoneType

directive.implicit_directive: Instead of hardcoding the device, use the to method to move the tensor to the device that is available or desired (i.e., device that other tensors are on).

api_misuse.explanation: Instead of hardcoding the device, use the to method to move the tensor to the device that other tensors are on.

524.impact: RuntimeError: Found no NVIDIA driver on your system. Please check that you have an NVIDIA GPU and installed a driver from http://www.nvidia.com/Download/index.aspx

directive.implicit_directive: Default value of dim is 0 which concatenates the tensors along the first dimension.

api_misuse.explanation: Default value of dim is 0, which may not be the desired results expected by the subseuqent operations. Explicitly set the dim parameter to avoid unexpected results or runtime errors.

674.impact: RuntimeError: mat1 and mat2 shapes cannot be multiplied (40x5 and 10x5)

directive.implicit_directive: Cast data to the same type before concatanating tensors.

api_misuse.explanation: When concatanating tensors, the tensors should have the same data type. Use tf.cast to convert the data type of the tensor.

937.impact: InvalidArgumentError: cannot compute ConcatV2 as input #1(zero-based) was expected to be a int32 tensor but is a int64 tensor [Op:ConcatV2] name: concat

directive.explicit_directive: the desired device of returned tensor. Default: if None, uses the current device for the default tensor type (see torch.set_default_device()). device will be the CPU for CPU tensor types and the current CUDA device for CUDA tensor types.

api_misuse.explanation: Set the device explicitly when performing operations on tensors to avoid device management errors.

1083.impact: RuntimeError: Expected all tensors to be on the same device, but found at least two devices, cuda:0 and cpu!

directive.explicit_directive: Unlike expand(), this function copies the tensor’s data.

api_misuse.explanation: When the dimension to expand is of size 1, using torch.expand(...) is doing exactly the same as torch.repeat(...) but without using extra memory.

directive.implicit_directive: To avoid data overflow errors, check the dtype of the tensor and change the value accordingly.

api_misuse.explanation: To avoid data overflow errors, set the fill value of the mask based on the dtype of the tensor.

1988.impact: RuntimeError: value cannot be converted to type at::Half without overflow

directive.implicit_directive: Use the sigmoid function to calculate the probability of the output for binary classification.

api_misuse.explanation: The training uses the BCEWithLogitsLoss, which means it internally calculates the probability using the sigmoid function. Therefore, explicitly use the sigmoid function in the evaluation step.

directive.explicit_directive: the desired device of returned tensor. Default: if None, uses the current device for the default tensor type (see torch.set_default_device()). device will be the CPU for CPU tensor types and the current CUDA device for CUDA tensor types.

api_misuse.explanation: Set the device explicitly when performing operations on tensors to avoid device management errors.

3531.impact: RuntimeError: Expected all tensors to be on the same device, but found at least two devices, cuda:0 and cpu!

directive.explicit_directive: the input tensor, either of floating point or complex dtype

api_misuse.explanation: mean expectes a tensor, not a list. Stack the tensor in the list to create a tensor.

3686.impact: TypeError: mean() received an invalid combination of arguments - got (list, dim=int), but expected one of:

directive.implicit_directive: To avoid data conversion errors, check the dtype of the tensor and change the type accordingly.

api_misuse.explanation: Tensors should be in the same type for multiplication.

4276.impact: RuntimeError: mat1 and mat2 must have the same dtype, but got Half and Float

directive.implicit_directive: Set the device explictly to avoid device management errors.

api_misuse.explanation: When creating tensors, set the device explicitly to make sure both matrices are on the same devices before multiplication.

5704.impact: RuntimeError: Expected all tensors to be on the same device, but found at least two devices, cpu and cuda:0!

directive.implicit_directive: Check if the loss value is NaN and raise an error if it is.

api_misuse.explanation: If the loss is NaN, the training process will not converge.
